framework.name: MAESTRO Agent-Specific Threat Taxonomy
framework.description: CSA's MAESTRO framework identifies threats STRIDE misses: emergent agent collusion, reputation exploitation, policy reinterpretation by LLMs, and observability bypass via emergent behaviors. Key insight for gap_6: the liability surface isn't just 'agent makes bad purchase' — it's agents collectively gaming systems in ways no single party authorized.
framework.raw_quote: Emergent Ecosystem Collusion: Multiple agents coordinating to manipulate markets (e.g., inflating prices via puppeteered negotiations)

source.title: Secure Use of the Agent Payments Protocol (AP2)
